rulings.Case Title: IVAN MERL BARNEY VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Establishing Admissions To And To Compel Verified Responses Written Discovery And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, November 7, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Dana Companies LLC¿s motion to deem requests for admissions admitted is granted. Plaintiffs failed to serve timely responses and have not served prior to the hearing a proposed response that is in substantial compliance with C.C.P. 2033.220. (C.C.P. 2033.280.) Defendant¿s motion to compel verified responses, without objections, to its special interrogatories, form interrogatories and requests for production is granted. Plaintiffs failed to serve timely responses and thus waived any objections. (C.C.P. 2030.290, 2031.300.) Plaintiffs have 15 days to serve verified responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories, form interrogatories and requests for production. Defendant¿s request for sanctions is awarded in the amount of $2287. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
